card_description: Moses (Hebrew/Egyptian: "Drawn from water") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the biblical tradition, Moses is lawgiver and prophet who led exodus. Referenced in PKD's discussions of divine law and theophany. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Moses. Context: "Dick identifies his experience as a literal "Passover Epiphany" occurring in August 1974, coinciding with Nixon’s resignation."
